item.content:encoded:
Black Friday and Cyber Monday (BFCM) deals have already started to fill our online feeds. But, I'm still waiting another week for every single deal to be revealed, so I can build the gaming PC that I've had my eyes on for almost a year now. Considering many PC components have been at all-time low prices, I'm excited to put together an all-white gaming PC — with a little extra boost from BFCM deals.

Now, this will not be a super value-for-money build, as my focus is on balancing performance as well as aesthetics. But, for around $2,000, and hopefully, even less around Black Friday, this all-white beauty is going to cut through any game at 1440p maxed-out settings, considering it features one of the only GPUs you should buy for Cyberpunk 2077's path tracing mode.

  • Source: AMD
    AMD Ryzen 5 7600
    Best CPU
    $226 at Amazon
  • Source: Newegg
    DeepCool LS720 SE Liquid Cooler White
    Best CPU cooler
    $98 at Amazon
  • Source: Best Buy
    Gigabyte GeForce RTX 4070 Ti AERO OC V2
    Best GPU
    $880 at Amazon
  • Source: Gigabyte
    Gigabyte B650 Aero G
    Best motherboard
    $160 at Amazon
  • Source: Newegg
    Corsair Vengeance RGB DDR5-6000 CL30 32GB (2x16GB)
    Best RAM
    $130 at Amazon
  • Samsung 990 PRO SSD 2TB
    Best SSD
    $130 at Amazon
  • Corsair RM850e
    Best PSU
    $115 at Newegg
  • Source: Lian Li
    Lian Li O11 Dynamic EVO
    Best case
    $149 at Newegg
  • Source: Amazon
    Corsair iCUE AR120 RGB Triple Fan Kit
    Best fans
    $45 at Amazon

Best $2,000 all-white gaming build with BFCM deals

Source: AMD
AMD Ryzen 5 7600
Best CPU

Best AM5 pick for gaming

This 6-core entry from AMD is one of the best gaming CPUs on the market, especially on the AM5 platform. Offering nearly the same performance as the Ryzen 5 7600X, you can boost performance even further by enabling PBO.

$226 at Amazon $229 at Best Buy $229 at Newegg

The Ryzen 5 7600 is an extremely popular 6-core chip from AMD's Ryzen 7000 series. It doesn't cost a lot, comes with a more than decent stock cooler, and can perform as well as or even better than the higher-clocked Ryzen 5 7600X. Enabling Precision Boost Overdrive (PBO) on the 7600 is an easy, one-click method to boost performance without messing around with any other overclocking settings, making this one of the best gaming CPUs available.

Going Intel will get you more performance but that would also mean spending at least $60 more for the Core i5-13600K for not that much more performance. Besides, if you're only concerned about gaming FPS, you don't need anything more powerful than the Ryzen 5 7600, except if you make a significant jump to the Core i7-13700K, Core i7-14700K, or the Ryzen 7 7800X3D for double the money and less than 50% more performance in many titles. More importantly, this would make it harder to accommodate other, white-themed components of the build.

Moreover, building on the new AM5 platform will offer you drop-in CPU upgrades for at least three more years if AMD's promises hold true. This would save you the cost of buying a new motherboard when you eventually upgrade your CPU. Come Black Friday, I'm expecting the Ryzen 5 7600 to drop to $200 at least.

Source: Newegg
DeepCool LS720 SE Liquid Cooler White
Best CPU cooler

Liquid cooling at a great price

$98 $110 Save $12

DeepCool's LS720 SE is known for its more-than-impressive cooling performance at a killer price. The white edition with its infinity mirror pump and ARGB fans make it a highly desirable option for budget as well as premium builds.

$98 at Amazon $98 at Newegg

Choosing a liquid cooler for this all-white build is one of the more important decisions, as the AIO pump as well as the fans occupy a prominent position in the build. Picking a good-looking AIO, hence, becomes important. Thankfully, you don't need to spend more than $100 for one of the best liquid coolers that performs as well as it looks. The DeepCool LS720 SE White liquid cooler offers cooling performance that punches above its weight. And the infinity mirror pump in white looks simply irresistible.

This cooler can easily handle CPUs with way higher power consumption than that of the Ryzen 5 7600. The 65W TDP of the 6-core Ryzen chip is easily tamed by DeepCool's 360mm radiator, but you do get the additional benefits of silent operation and a clean look by opting for this liquid AIO. And at a time when the LS720 SE is going for its lowest price ever, it's an easy recommendation.

Source: Best Buy
Gigabyte GeForce RTX 4070 Ti AERO OC V2
Best GPU

White-hot performance and design

For the centerpiece of this all-white gaming PC, the Gigabyte GeForce RTX 4070 Ti AERO OC V2 is easily the best choice. It combines the exceptional 1440p performance of the RTX 4070 Ti with a minimal, all-white design that adds several layers to the looks of your build.

$880 at Amazon $880 at Best Buy $880 at Newegg

White graphics cards might not be a rarity anymore, what with every major AIB producing at least one white-themed variant for nearly every GPU. But, choosing one that fits your budget, performance requirement, as well as design sensibilities, is not always easy. The Gigabyte GeForce RTX 4070 Ti AERO OC V2, however, manages to tick all of my checkboxes for a 1440p maxed-out gaming PC.

Nailing a white design that is light on RGB, yet looks phenomenal is difficult. But the Gigabyte AERO design manages to keep things subtle while still attracting every stare toward itself. Your GPU is bound to become the focus of your all-white build with its massive, triple-fan cooling system that keeps things silent and comfy under the heaviest loads. And you'll have no issues playing anything on the maximum settings at 1440p resolution. Even at 4K, with a few settings dialed down, you can enjoy a 60+ FPS experience, thanks to Nvidia's Frame Generation and DLSS 3.5 upscaling.

Being one of the best GPUs in 2023, the RTX 4070 Ti is obviously no slouch. But coupled with Gigabyte's swanky white design for a small premium, you can instantly give your build a massive facelift. If you wait till Black Friday, you might just save around $30-$50 more on this brilliant graphics card.

Source: Gigabyte
Gigabyte B650 Aero G
Best motherboard

Great looks, features, and performance

$160 $270 Save $110

The Gigabyte B650 AERO G offers solid value for a sub-$200 B650 motherboard. Whether it's PCIe 5.0 M.2 slots, Wi-Fi 6E, or the 12+2 VRM design, this motherboard comes ready for all of your gaming and overclocking needs. The eye-catching white-themed design complements the build perfectly.

$160 at Amazon $160 at Newegg

Finding an all-white motherboard can be hard, considering you can never really escape the black PCB and a few other elements. But, the Gigabyte B650 AERO G is one of the best gaming motherboards that incorporates the white theme brilliantly, without making you spend upwards of $300 for a B650E model (looking at you, NZXT). As for performance and features, this Gigabyte model doesn't really lack much.

You're getting a Gen5 M.2 slot, along with two more Gen4 slots for additional storage. The 12+2 VRM design on this ATX board is more than capable of any overclocking you'll do on the Ryzen 5 7600 or any other high-end CPU in the future. Plus, you're getting Wi-Fi 6E and heatsinks for all of the M.2 slots — again, in white. The I/O panel at the back features plenty of connectivity options, and the board overall even has some subtle RGB, so you don't miss out on those extra FPS.

This motherboard is currently selling for around $190 but could go down to $160 during Black Friday or Cyber Monday, based on its price history. Wait for a week, and you could grab a great deal on one of the best-looking B650 motherboards on the market.

Source: Newegg
Corsair Vengeance RGB DDR5-6000 CL30 32GB (2x16GB)
Best RAM

Extremely fast and enviably dazzling

The Corsair Vengeance RGB series offers the best of both worlds — peak performance and gorgeous design. The DDR5-6000 CL30 32GB kit comes with Corsair's renowned performance and stability, with an RGB-laden white design that's unparalleled on the market.

$130 at Amazon $130 at Newegg

When building on AMD's AM5 platform, the RAM and motherboard costs used to be sky-high. But in the past year, prices have fallen drastically, to the point where you can get a 32GB RGB kit running at 6000MT/s for less than $100. For the specific kit in the build, however, you'll need to spend a fair bit more if you want the absolute best-looking DIMMs with the speed and latency that's the sweet spot for Ryzen.

The Corsair Vengeance RGB DDR5-6000 CL30 32GB (2x16GB) kit costs around 20-30% more than various competitors, but you get one of the best DDR5 RAM on the market, both in terms of performance and looks. The Vengeance RGB white RAM is my absolute favorite RGB design among some strong contenders. It doesn't have the overly aggressive "gaming" vibe, the white-gray theme is perfect for this build, and the RGB lighting is simply breathtaking.

What's more, you're getting 6000MT/s of CL30 speed for peak gaming performance. You can definitely get other white memory kits with the same specs at lower prices, but in my opinion, the Corsair Vengeance RGB kit is well worth the premium.

Samsung 990 PRO SSD 2TB
Best SSD

Performance champ at a fantastic price

$130 $137 Save $7

The 990 Pro from Samsung is one of the absolute fastest drives on the market, and coupled with Samsung's famed endurance and reliability, it's a no-brainer, especially at its current price.

$130 at Amazon $135 at Samsung $135 at Best Buy

You don't need to sacrifice storage performance for design as the motherboard's heatsink will completely cover your NVMe SSD. Hence, look no further than the Samsung 990 Pro 2TB for your dedicated OS as well as gaming drive. The 990 Pro has been ruling the best SSDs lists since forever now, owing to its blazing-fast performance, excellent reliability, and competitive pricing.

Even at around $130, it more than justifies its price, considering its place as an excellent gaming as well as productivity drive. Samsung's legendary reputation as well as an impressive software suite further cement the 990 Pro's position at the top of the best Gen 4 NVMe drives. Even though the motherboard in this build features a Gen 5 slot, the price-to-performance doesn't justify jumping to PCIe 5.0 SSDs for almost double the price.

Lastly, a 2TB drive should be plenty big for storing your games as well as other media and software apps. And you could always opt for an additional 1TB or 2TB NVMe drive in the future. BFCM deals are likely to bring the 990 Pro down to around $120 or even $110, saving you even more on an excellent product.

Corsair RM850e
Best PSU

850W of premium, future-ready power

$115 $130 Save $15

Corsair's RM850e is a future-ready 80+ Gold power supply for safeguarding your precious hardware. It's fully modular, extremely reliable, and compliant with ATX 3.0 and PCIe 5.0 standards, ensuring the best performance for your high-end gaming PC.

$120 at Amazon $115 at Newegg

When it comes to choosing the best PSU for gaming, you might consider wattage and price alone. But the power supply is one of the areas where you should never cut costs. In fact, it's much more preferable to buy a better-quality 750W unit instead of a cheap 1000W one. In the case of the Corsair RM850e, you don't need to choose between price and quality. For what it offers, the RM850e is priced competitively, to say the least.

Corsair's RM series is known for its top-tier quality and efficiency. The RM850e is no different, offering 850W of total power draw along with 80+ Gold efficiency. It's fully modular and is compliant with the latest ATX 3.0 and PCIe 5.0 standards. The black design will not disturb your all-white theme as the case I've chosen will hide the PSU from view. Although a decent 750W unit is more than enough for an RTX 4070 Ti build, 850W will allow you room for future upgrades.

You could find other reliable 850W units for $5-10 less, but there's no need to pinch pennies in this department. The Corsair RM850e is easily one of the most quality power supplies on the market, and with its forward-looking specs, it's ideal for any new builder.

Source: Lian Li
Lian Li O11 Dynamic EVO
Best case

The best case for white builds

$149 $170 Save $21

An all-white gaming PC deserves a standout all-white case. The Lian Li O11 Dynamic EVO is still one of the best PC cases for showing off your premium, white-themed build, featuring best-in-class space, flexibility, and room.

$149 at Newegg $150 at Walmart

Besides the graphics card, the case is the component that contributes the most to the overall look of your PC. And for a build focused so much on an all-white theme, you can't do better than the Lian Li O11 Dynamic EVO. Still one of the best PC cases, and certainly the most popular among gamers, the O11 Dynamic EVO is just too good — you're getting all the flexibility you could need, ample cable management and airflow, and a stunning all-white glass design.

While it's not what you would call competitive, considering its relatively high price tag, it's not a huge downside if looks are important to you. Plus, the degree of freedom it provides with its reversible layout and changeable I/O port is rarely seen elsewhere. The massive volume of the case gives your beautiful components enough room to be fully appreciated. And the performance isn't sacrificed either, as the top and side mesh panels provide a great amount of airflow.

You can easily mount three separate 360mm radiators at the top, front, and bottom of the case, with support for a 120mm fan at the rear as well. The O11 Dynamic EVO does justice to an all-white build and is one of the most stunning white PC cases you can buy.

Source: Amazon
Corsair iCUE AR120 RGB Triple Fan Kit
Best fans

Great looks, great performance

$45 $55 Save $10

Corsair's iCUE AR120 RGB fans are some of the best-looking case fans available. For your all-white gaming PC, you won't be disappointed with their cooling performance either. The triple pack comes with an ARGB adapter for easily syncing them with your RGB theme.

$45 at Amazon $45 at Newegg

To really complete the overall look of your all-white build, you'll need additional case fans as the O11 Dynamic EVO doesn't come with bundled fans. The Corsair iCUE AR120 RGB fans are some of the best ones for the job as they combine great looks and decent performance into a relatively affordable package. You'll need two triple packs — three for side intake and three for bottom intake, plus a single pack to take care of the rear exhaust.

In total, the seven fans would cost you around $115. This might sound like a lot, but when you consider the value they bring, both in terms of airflow and looks, it begins to make a lot more sense. Besides, even some of the best PC case fans don't look as good as the AR120s, while costing significantly more than them. Corsair's iCUE software simplifies syncing the RGB lighting of the fans with the overall theme of your build.

Wait for BFCM deals to build this all-white gaming PC

This all-white $2,000 gaming PC is sure to be a 1440p monster, and it'll look like a dream at the same time. But, if you're able to wait another week, you can snag at least $100-150 off of this price with some unbeatable deals that are sure to go live as part of Black Friday and Cyber Monday. If not, this is still a great build at the current prices. And, if you're more into pre-builds, you can browse the best gaming PCs on the market that are having some unmissable deals right now.

item.title: Best Amazon Echo devices in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best Amazon Echo devices in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
It’s been a couple of months since Amazon’s latest event, where the company announced new and exciting products that deserve to be part of our selection of the best Amazon Echo devices in 2023. These new products arrive with some necessary upgrades and cool features that make us love our Echo devices a bit more. The best part is that the company aims to please and provide an excellent experience with each of its devices. There are tons of options to choose from, so getting the best Echo for you might seem like a complicated task if it’s your first time, but once you get a feel for the products and services, you will feel the need to add more devices to your collection to create a better smart home experience. So, which is the best Echo device for you or the best addition to your smart home? Stick around to find out.

  • Amazon Echo (4th Gen)
    Best overall
    $55 at Amazon
  • Source: Amazon
    Amazon Echo Dot (5th Gen)
    Best for beginners
    $23 at Amazon
  • Amazon Echo Dot Kids (5th Gen)
    Best for kids
    $28 at Amazon
  • Amazon Echo Studio
    Best for sound
    $155 at Amazon
  • Amazon Echo Show 8 (3rd Gen, 2023)
    Best smart display
    $105 at Amazon
  • Source: Amazon
    Echo Show 5 (3rd Gen)
    Best for your nightstand
    $40 at Amazon
  • Echo Auto (2nd Gen)
    Best for your car
    $35 at Amazon
  • Source: Amazon
    Amazon Echo Pop
    Best for small spaces
    $18 at Amazon
  • Echo Buds (2nd Gen)
    Best headphones
    $65 at Amazon
  • Echo Glow
    Best Night light
    $17 at Amazon

Our favorite Amazon Echo devices in 2023

Amazon Echo (4th Gen)
Best overall

An all-rounder with good sound and Alexa

$55 $100 Save $45

Amazon’s Echo smart speaker may be the best option for those looking to have good sound, and a minimalist design that will blend well with most of your decoration, as it comes with three different color options.

Pros
  • Great audio output with reliable mic
  • Smart home compatibility
  • Supports Zigbee
Cons
  • No LED clock option
  • Design may not be for everyone
  • Top volume will distort your tunes
$55 at Amazon

Amazon’s latest iteration of the Echo smart speaker is still our top pick, as Amazon seems to be more than pleased with its performance and what it offers to its customers. It is a relatively large smart speaker, but it will blend perfectly with any kind of decoration you may want to use in your room or living room. Plus, it’s also one of the most reliable options for those interested in picking up a new Echo device. It will deliver great sound by itself but remember that you can take your media experience to new heights when you pair your Echo with a couple of Echo Dots to experience room-filling stereo sound.

There are several things to love here, starting with a spherical design that fits all the necessary components to produce great sound while keeping an affordable price tag. Color options are also great, even though I’m still trying to understand why Amazon included a white power cable with every color variant. Control buttons are also easily accessible, with a physical button that will turn off the mic in case you want added privacy. You also get a 3.5mm line to connect any device and play your favorite tunes even without Wi-Fi connectivity.

However, one of the best features of Echo devices is Alexa, which is one of the best digital assistants. Alexa will let you control your lights, music, and other compatible devices with simple voice commands. The Echo is also easy to control via the Alexa app, where you can pair it with your devices, create routines, groups, and more. But one of the things I love the most about this speaker is that I can pair it with my TV and other Echo speakers to create a practical and effective home theater experience without having to pay extra for a soundbar or other more expensive speakers.

Finally, the Echo comes with a built-in Zigbee smart hub, which doesn't require a data connection to connect with compatible devices like the Philips Hue lights, for instance. Alexa is still great and will work as advertised, but having a built-in Zigbee hub makes it much more versatile.

Source: Amazon
Amazon Echo Dot (5th Gen)
Best for beginners

Good to test the waters with Alexa

$23 $50 Save $27

The Amazon Echo Dot (5th Gen) boasts of improved audio quality with clearer vocals and better bass.

Pros
  • Built-in motion and temperature sensors
  • Good audio output for the price and its size
  • Color and cute stand options
Cons
  • No more 3.5mm jack
  • Not the best sound
$23 at Amazon

Amazon's fifth-generation Echo Dot looks very similar to the previous model, but it comes bearing some solid improvements under the hood. It brings all the changes while retaining the same form factor and price that we know and love, so it gets an easy recommendation.

One of the biggest changes it brings is in the audio department. The Echo Dot (5th Gen) has a new speaker with a slightly larger driver — a 1.73-inch front-firing speaker instead of the 1.6-inch driver found inside the previous version. There are also new sensors, including a temperature sensor that allow it to read the temperature of the room, which it can use to automate Alexa Routines. For instance, you can set a Routine to turn on the fan or the air conditioner when the room gets to a certain temperature. Additionally, the new Echo Dot speakers also have an accelerometer to enable tap interactions.

Lastly, it's worth noting that the new Echo Dot speakers can also become a part of Eero networks to extend the coverage of the existing network across your home. This particular feature, however, isn't exclusive to these new Echo Dot speakers. Last-gen models are also said to receive this feature via an update in the future.

Amazon Echo Dot Kids (5th Gen)
Best for kids

The regular Echo Dot with a new skin

$28 $60 Save $32

Not only does the Echo Dot Kids Edition come with fun new designs, but it also brings child-friendly content and a parent-friendly warranty to the already-great Echo Dot and WiFi extending for Eero mesh.

Pros
  • Fun owl and dragon designs for kids
  • Can expand your Wi-Fi coverage
  • Same features from regular Echo Dot
Cons
  • Limited kid-friendly features outside Amazon Kids+
  • Only two designs to choose from
$28 at Amazon

If want to buy a smart speaker for your kids, then the Echo Dot Kids Edition (5th Gen) is a good option to consider. It costs $10 more than the regular Echo Dot, but it has the same hardware. You get all the new features and improvements, but with a fun owl or dragon print on the fabric, along with some kid-specific features.

For an additional cost, the Kids Edition comes with an Amazon Parent dashboard with parental controls. You also get a two-year guarantee, just like with Amazon Fire Kids tablets, so you can get a no-questions-asked replacement in case your kids decide to, you know, use the Echo Dot as a ball. Additionally, you also get a year's subscription to Amazon Kids+ with a collection of kid-friendly filtered content.

Amazon says it's working around the clock to make Alexa more kid-friendly. You may hear some custom responses from Alexa, but the overall experience is largely going to be the same. Amazon has made it a point to disable some standard Alexa features on the Kids Edition, though, including voice purchasing to keep your kids from placing random orders of toys and games. Plus, explicit lyrics from Amazon Music, Apple Music, or Spotify are blocked by default.

Overall, the Echo Dot Kids Edition (5th Gen) is a great smart speaker for kids. Whether you want your younglings to have a smart speaker in their room is entirely up to you.

Amazon Echo Studio
Best for sound

One of the best-sounding Echo speakers out there

$155 $200 Save $45

The Echo Studio is the best smart speaker that Amazon has to offer. The speaker can be purchased in Charcoal and the all-new Glacier color.

Pros
  • Impressive audio quality
  • Supports Zigbee hub
Cons
  • Large footprint
  • Expensive price tag
$155 at Amazon

Amazon's current Echo speakers all sound good, but if you truly want to enjoy listening to music, then you should consider buying the Echo Studio. Yes, it's due for an update to compete against other premium speakers, but it still packs enough punch to pump out the best audio in the Echo lineup, including the Echo Sub.

The Echo Studio stands roughly 8 inches tall and about 7 inches wide. It's also a fabric-covered cylinder, just like the Echo Sub that came out a year before. The Studio, however, packs better five audio drivers, including a 5.25-inch downward-firing woofer, a one-inch forward-facing tweeter, and three two-inch midrange speakers firing audio on the left, right, and top. All these drivers are powered by a 330W amp with a 24-bit DAC.

One of the best things about the Echo Studio is that it's a standalone speaker that works independently without relying on other Echo speakers, unlike the Echo Sub. It comes with full support for Alexa, meaning you can control your smart home devices or play music and get other information with just your voice. The Echo Studio even has the Zigbee hub built in that lets you directly connect smart home devices.

Amazon Echo Show 8 (3rd Gen, 2023)
Best smart display

Perfect size, solid features

$105 $150 Save $45

Amazon's third-generation Echo Show 8 is a new and powerful smart display that features a better display, improved audio, and all the features you get with the Alexa digital assistant that will let you control your smart home via easy voice commands.

Pros
  • Improved audio with Spatial Audio
  • Faster processor
Cons
  • A bit expensive for most
$105 at Amazon

The Echo Show 8 is one of my favorite Echo products, and it was one of the ones that received a very important upgrade, as this new smart display now includes excellent features that set it apart from other smart displays on the market. The third-generation Echo Show 8 now arrives with improved audio performance thanks to spatial audio, room acoustic sensing, and noise reduction technology that will make it sound better than ever before.

The Echo Show 8 also comes with an 8-inch HD touchscreen and a 13MP camera that will make your video calls more enjoyable. Also, you get a built-in smart home hub for seamless connectivity with Alexa-compatible devices and products, meaning that you can easily connect and control your lights, alarms, cameras, smart TVs, and more. It also features an improved processor with faster responses and an updated design with edge-to-edge glass and a centered camera. It also comes with a new $150 price tag, but it’s worth every penny.

Remember that the Echo Show 8 will also connect directly to other Echo devices to deliver a better media experience, with room-filling sound and two different color options to choose from. And it comes with multiple layers of privacy controls, including a mic/camera off button and a built-in camera shutter for ease of mind.

Source: Amazon
Echo Show 5 (3rd Gen)
Best for your nightstand

Bedside table smart display with Alexa

$40 $90 Save $50

The third-generation Amazon Echo Show 5 is one of Amazon's latest devices, which comes with several upgrades that make it the perfect companion for your bedside table.

Pros
  • Compact design
  • Improved audio and display
  • An affordable smart display
Cons
  • Not the best for video calls
$40 at Amazon

The Echo Show 5 is another device that received a facelift and some other improvements. This third-generation smart display is perfect for your nightstand. It comes with a more budget-friendly price tag, and a smaller 5.5-inch display that will let you check your notifications, and any other info that’s relevant for you. And don’t be fooled by its size, as it is a small but mighty smart display that will let you do everything you can do with its larger variant thanks to Alexa.

Amazon’s new Echo Show 5 comes in three different color variations, and you can even get yours with an adjustable stand if you’re willing to pay a bit more. This smart display comes with improved audio and better voice clarity, deeper bass, additional microphone for improved Alexa responsiveness, a new and faster processor with the company’s Az22 Neural Edge, an improved screen for a better viewing experience at night. And you can also pick up the kids variant that comes with extra security features to keep your kids safe and under control.

Echo Auto (2nd Gen)
Best for your car

Don’t leave home without Alexa

$35 $55 Save $20

Most cars today arrive with great tools and features that make them better and smarter than before, but it’s always great to have Alexa ride with you so you can keep tabs on everything.
 

Pros
  • Smaller than its predecessor
  • Easy to install
  • Alexa on the go
Cons
  • Doesn't offer new features
  • Uses your phone's data plan
$35 at Amazon

The Echo Auto may not seem like the best option for those with newer cars since these vehicles usually arrive with tech like Android Auto and CarPlay that give you access to voice commands and touch-sensitive screens to interact with apps. However, there are plenty of vehicles that don’t include any of those features and might not even have Bluetooth support, which is exactly why Amazon Echo Auto is a great option. It immediately makes your car smarter by adding Alexa support and some convenient voice commands to your ride.

The new Echo Auto is not a smart speaker; it just comes with a microphone that connects to your car. Still, it will make your ride smarter. You can tell Alexa to play your favorite music, keep you updated with the latest news and sports scores, and even control your devices at home, meaning you can easily turn on or off your lights while on the road. It's quite convenient if you’re used to using Alexa as your main digital assistant.

You can easily set it up via Bluetooth connection or with the auxiliary cable that comes with your purchase. This will also allow you to connect Alexa with Android Auto or Apple’s CarPlay. Then it will ask you to configure different functions like calls, texts, directions, and media, which is great if you’re subscribed to Amazon’s Prime services, Spotify, and more.

I use mine to ask Alexa to play my favorite tunes, read my notifications, and add stuff to my shopping list. Still, there are many other ways to take advantage of Alexa in your car, as it continues to be one of the best and most efficient digital assistants you can use. Just don’t get too upset if your device loses connectivity while hitting the road, as data will be affected according to your company’s coverage.

Source: Amazon
Amazon Echo Pop
Best for small spaces

It will fit anywhere you want

$18 $40 Save $22

Amazon's smallest smart speaker will be perfect for small rooms and tight spaces as it won't take up too much space and will still deliver nice audio.

Pros
  • Small and compact footprint
  • Several color options
Cons
  • Not the best audio quality
  • Would've loved color-matching power cable
$18 at Amazon

Amazon’s most affordable smart speaker comes with a highly compact design that makes it perfect for any tight spot. It comes in four different color variants, and its $40 price tag makes it also the company’s most affordable option for those interested in having a first experience with Alexa’s ecosystem. You will also receive excellent audio that will be great for bedrooms and small spaces, and Alexa support will also let you control your favorite tunes from your preferred music streaming services. You can also connect to this speaker via Bluetooth in case you want to stream directly to your speaker.

Amazon Echo Pop will also let you control your smart home devices, so you can ask Alexa to clean your room if you have an Alexa-compatible robot vacuum or tell her to turn off the lights before you go to bed. And since we’re talking about lights, you will also be pleased to know that this smart speaker will only allow Alexa to listen to your queries and commands until it hears you say “Alexa” and the light turns blue. You can also turn off the mic on your smart speaker with a physical off button for added privacy.

Echo Buds (2nd Gen)
Best headphones

Affordable audio you can take anywhere you go

$65 $120 Save $55

Amazon's Echo Buds with active noise canceling are an excellent option for anyone who wants great audio and long-lasting battery life on a budget. 

Pros
  • Great audio and noise cancelling
  • Decent battery life
Cons
  • Registration with Alexa app could be optional
$65 at Amazon

There are tons of amazing wireless earbuds on the market, but Amazon has a great pair as well, as the company’s Echo Buds with active noise canceling will deliver excellent audio quality without breaking the bank. These wireless earbuds were launched back in 2021, which also means that they’ve become more affordable with time, making them more compelling for those interested in picking up a pair of noise-canceling earbuds with Alexa support.

The Echo Buds with Active Noise Cancellation launched with a $120 price tag, but you can now easily pick up a pair for less than $70 with Amazon’s constant offers. Either way, you would be taking an excellent product with premium speakers to deliver crisp, balanced sound, and you can also personalize your audio for a unique hearing experience. They’re also quite compact and easy to wear, so you won’t have issues using them for extended periods of time, which will be limited to five hours of continuous music playback. You can also quickly charge them to get up to two hours of playback after a short 15-minute charge.

The best part about these wireless earbuds is that they are compatible with iOS and Android devices. You will only need to have a device with Android 8 or iOS 14 or later and activate them in the Alexa app, where you will also be able to mute the mics to keep your earbuds from listening to anything you have to say.

Echo Glow
Best Night light
$17 $30 Save $13

Amazon's Echo Glow will require a compatible Echo smart speaker to shine. Control your brightness, colors, and more via voice control to give your kids one of the best night lamps on the market.

Pros
  • Compact design with bright lights
  • Your kids will love it
Cons
  • Needs a compatible Echo smart speaker to work
  • No music sync
$17 at Amazon

The Echo Glow is another excellent product from Amazon, but it’s one of the few devices that needs another Echo device to work as intended. The Echo Glow is a multicolor bright lamp that requires a compatible Alexa device to operate. It is compatible with basically everything that came after the third-generation Echo Dot smart speaker. You will use your smart speaker to turn the Echo Glow on or off, control the color and brightness of your lamp with voice commands, and make it the perfect companion for your kids.

You can also create helpful routines reminding you to go to bed, take your pills, or start cooking when colors change. Setting a rainbow timer is also a nice option to give your kids time to get ready for school or to get them to do anything else. It comes with a compact footprint that will make it easier for you to set it up anywhere you want, and it also features an action button on the back that will let you interact with this fun little night light. I would only recommend you wait for Black Friday savings or Prime Day deals to pick one up, as paying $30 for a night light might not be for everyone.

Best Amazon Echo devices in 2023: Final thoughts

Amazon, as we mentioned earlier, has a lot of Echo devices in its lineup, but we've managed to highlight the best ones you can consider right now. Out of all the options mentioned in this collection, we'd say the Amazon Echo (4th Gen) is the best for most users. It's a great-sounding speaker that can fill your room with good music, and it also supports Alexa to control your smart home devices. Amazon has yet to release an updated fifth-generation model of the regular Echo speaker, so this one remains our best pick for now.

Amazon Echo (4th Gen)
Best overall
$55 $100 Save $45

Amazon's 4th Echo speaker remains our pick for the best overall Echo device you can buy right now. It's due for an upgrade this year, but you can't go wrong with this model too.

$55 at Amazon

Those who are relatively new to the world of Amazon's Echo speakers should consider buying the Echo Dot (5th Gen). It's a solid speaker to kick things off, and it's particularly good when you pair it with a couple of smart home products like smart bulbs and start playing with the idea of controlling your devices with an Echo before upgrading to a bigger, more expensive speaker.

It’s been a couple of months since Amazon’s latest event, where the company announced new and exciting products that deserve to be part of our selection of the best Amazon Echo devices in 2023. These new products arrive with some necessary upgrades and cool features that make us love our Echo devices a bit more. The best part is that the company aims to please and provide an excellent experience with each of its devices. There are tons of options to choose from, so getting the best Echo for you might seem like a complicated task if it’s your first time, but once you get a feel for the products and services, you will feel the need to add more devices to your collection to create a better smart home experience. So, which is the best Echo device for you or the best addition to your smart home? Stick around to find out.

  • Amazon Echo (4th Gen)
    Best overall
    $55 at Amazon
  • Source: Amazon
    Amazon Echo Dot (5th Gen)
    Best for beginners
    $23 at Amazon
  • Amazon Echo Dot Kids (5th Gen)
    Best for kids
    $28 at Amazon
  • Amazon Echo Studio
    Best for sound
    $155 at Amazon
  • Amazon Echo Show 8 (3rd Gen, 2023)
    Best smart display
    $105 at Amazon
  • Source: Amazon
    Echo Show 5 (3rd Gen)
    Best for your nightstand
    $40 at Amazon
  • Echo Auto (2nd Gen)
    Best for your car
    $35 at Amazon
  • Source: Amazon
    Amazon Echo Pop
    Best for small spaces
    $18 at Amazon
  • Echo Buds (2nd Gen)
    Best headphones
    $65 at Amazon
  • Echo Glow
    Best Night light
    $17 at Amazon

Our favorite Amazon Echo devices in 2023

Amazon Echo (4th Gen)
Best overall

An all-rounder with good sound and Alexa

$55 $100 Save $45

Amazon’s Echo smart speaker may be the best option for those looking to have good sound, and a minimalist design that will blend well with most of your decoration, as it comes with three different color options.

Pros
  • Great audio output with reliable mic
  • Smart home compatibility
  • Supports Zigbee
Cons
  • No LED clock option
  • Design may not be for everyone
  • Top volume will distort your tunes
$55 at Amazon

Amazon’s latest iteration of the Echo smart speaker is still our top pick, as Amazon seems to be more than pleased with its performance and what it offers to its customers. It is a relatively large smart speaker, but it will blend perfectly with any kind of decoration you may want to use in your room or living room. Plus, it’s also one of the most reliable options for those interested in picking up a new Echo device. It will deliver great sound by itself but remember that you can take your media experience to new heights when you pair your Echo with a couple of Echo Dots to experience room-filling stereo sound.

There are several things to love here, starting with a spherical design that fits all the necessary components to produce great sound while keeping an affordable price tag. Color options are also great, even though I’m still trying to understand why Amazon included a white power cable with every color variant. Control buttons are also easily accessible, with a physical button that will turn off the mic in case you want added privacy. You also get a 3.5mm line to connect any device and play your favorite tunes even without Wi-Fi connectivity.

However, one of the best features of Echo devices is Alexa, which is one of the best digital assistants. Alexa will let you control your lights, music, and other compatible devices with simple voice commands. The Echo is also easy to control via the Alexa app, where you can pair it with your devices, create routines, groups, and more. But one of the things I love the most about this speaker is that I can pair it with my TV and other Echo speakers to create a practical and effective home theater experience without having to pay extra for a soundbar or other more expensive speakers.

Finally, the Echo comes with a built-in Zigbee smart hub, which doesn't require a data connection to connect with compatible devices like the Philips Hue lights, for instance. Alexa is still great and will work as advertised, but having a built-in Zigbee hub makes it much more versatile.

Source: Amazon
Amazon Echo Dot (5th Gen)
Best for beginners

Good to test the waters with Alexa

$23 $50 Save $27

The Amazon Echo Dot (5th Gen) boasts of improved audio quality with clearer vocals and better bass.

Pros
  • Built-in motion and temperature sensors
  • Good audio output for the price and its size
  • Color and cute stand options
Cons
  • No more 3.5mm jack
  • Not the best sound
$23 at Amazon

Amazon's fifth-generation Echo Dot looks very similar to the previous model, but it comes bearing some solid improvements under the hood. It brings all the changes while retaining the same form factor and price that we know and love, so it gets an easy recommendation.

One of the biggest changes it brings is in the audio department. The Echo Dot (5th Gen) has a new speaker with a slightly larger driver — a 1.73-inch front-firing speaker instead of the 1.6-inch driver found inside the previous version. There are also new sensors, including a temperature sensor that allow it to read the temperature of the room, which it can use to automate Alexa Routines. For instance, you can set a Routine to turn on the fan or the air conditioner when the room gets to a certain temperature. Additionally, the new Echo Dot speakers also have an accelerometer to enable tap interactions.

Lastly, it's worth noting that the new Echo Dot speakers can also become a part of Eero networks to extend the coverage of the existing network across your home. This particular feature, however, isn't exclusive to these new Echo Dot speakers. Last-gen models are also said to receive this feature via an update in the future.

Amazon Echo Dot Kids (5th Gen)
Best for kids

The regular Echo Dot with a new skin

$28 $60 Save $32

Not only does the Echo Dot Kids Edition come with fun new designs, but it also brings child-friendly content and a parent-friendly warranty to the already-great Echo Dot and WiFi extending for Eero mesh.

Pros
  • Fun owl and dragon designs for kids
  • Can expand your Wi-Fi coverage
  • Same features from regular Echo Dot
Cons
  • Limited kid-friendly features outside Amazon Kids+
  • Only two designs to choose from
$28 at Amazon

If want to buy a smart speaker for your kids, then the Echo Dot Kids Edition (5th Gen) is a good option to consider. It costs $10 more than the regular Echo Dot, but it has the same hardware. You get all the new features and improvements, but with a fun owl or dragon print on the fabric, along with some kid-specific features.

For an additional cost, the Kids Edition comes with an Amazon Parent dashboard with parental controls. You also get a two-year guarantee, just like with Amazon Fire Kids tablets, so you can get a no-questions-asked replacement in case your kids decide to, you know, use the Echo Dot as a ball. Additionally, you also get a year's subscription to Amazon Kids+ with a collection of kid-friendly filtered content.

Amazon says it's working around the clock to make Alexa more kid-friendly. You may hear some custom responses from Alexa, but the overall experience is largely going to be the same. Amazon has made it a point to disable some standard Alexa features on the Kids Edition, though, including voice purchasing to keep your kids from placing random orders of toys and games. Plus, explicit lyrics from Amazon Music, Apple Music, or Spotify are blocked by default.

Overall, the Echo Dot Kids Edition (5th Gen) is a great smart speaker for kids. Whether you want your younglings to have a smart speaker in their room is entirely up to you.

Amazon Echo Studio
Best for sound

One of the best-sounding Echo speakers out there

$155 $200 Save $45

The Echo Studio is the best smart speaker that Amazon has to offer. The speaker can be purchased in Charcoal and the all-new Glacier color.

Pros
  • Impressive audio quality
  • Supports Zigbee hub
Cons
  • Large footprint
  • Expensive price tag
$155 at Amazon

Amazon's current Echo speakers all sound good, but if you truly want to enjoy listening to music, then you should consider buying the Echo Studio. Yes, it's due for an update to compete against other premium speakers, but it still packs enough punch to pump out the best audio in the Echo lineup, including the Echo Sub.

The Echo Studio stands roughly 8 inches tall and about 7 inches wide. It's also a fabric-covered cylinder, just like the Echo Sub that came out a year before. The Studio, however, packs better five audio drivers, including a 5.25-inch downward-firing woofer, a one-inch forward-facing tweeter, and three two-inch midrange speakers firing audio on the left, right, and top. All these drivers are powered by a 330W amp with a 24-bit DAC.

One of the best things about the Echo Studio is that it's a standalone speaker that works independently without relying on other Echo speakers, unlike the Echo Sub. It comes with full support for Alexa, meaning you can control your smart home devices or play music and get other information with just your voice. The Echo Studio even has the Zigbee hub built in that lets you directly connect smart home devices.

Amazon Echo Show 8 (3rd Gen, 2023)
Best smart display

Perfect size, solid features

$105 $150 Save $45

Amazon's third-generation Echo Show 8 is a new and powerful smart display that features a better display, improved audio, and all the features you get with the Alexa digital assistant that will let you control your smart home via easy voice commands.

Pros
  • Improved audio with Spatial Audio
  • Faster processor
Cons
  • A bit expensive for most
$105 at Amazon

The Echo Show 8 is one of my favorite Echo products, and it was one of the ones that received a very important upgrade, as this new smart display now includes excellent features that set it apart from other smart displays on the market. The third-generation Echo Show 8 now arrives with improved audio performance thanks to spatial audio, room acoustic sensing, and noise reduction technology that will make it sound better than ever before.

The Echo Show 8 also comes with an 8-inch HD touchscreen and a 13MP camera that will make your video calls more enjoyable. Also, you get a built-in smart home hub for seamless connectivity with Alexa-compatible devices and products, meaning that you can easily connect and control your lights, alarms, cameras, smart TVs, and more. It also features an improved processor with faster responses and an updated design with edge-to-edge glass and a centered camera. It also comes with a new $150 price tag, but it’s worth every penny.

Remember that the Echo Show 8 will also connect directly to other Echo devices to deliver a better media experience, with room-filling sound and two different color options to choose from. And it comes with multiple layers of privacy controls, including a mic/camera off button and a built-in camera shutter for ease of mind.

Source: Amazon
Echo Show 5 (3rd Gen)
Best for your nightstand

Bedside table smart display with Alexa

$40 $90 Save $50

The third-generation Amazon Echo Show 5 is one of Amazon's latest devices, which comes with several upgrades that make it the perfect companion for your bedside table.

Pros
  • Compact design
  • Improved audio and display
  • An affordable smart display
Cons
  • Not the best for video calls
$40 at Amazon

The Echo Show 5 is another device that received a facelift and some other improvements. This third-generation smart display is perfect for your nightstand. It comes with a more budget-friendly price tag, and a smaller 5.5-inch display that will let you check your notifications, and any other info that’s relevant for you. And don’t be fooled by its size, as it is a small but mighty smart display that will let you do everything you can do with its larger variant thanks to Alexa.

Amazon’s new Echo Show 5 comes in three different color variations, and you can even get yours with an adjustable stand if you’re willing to pay a bit more. This smart display comes with improved audio and better voice clarity, deeper bass, additional microphone for improved Alexa responsiveness, a new and faster processor with the company’s Az22 Neural Edge, an improved screen for a better viewing experience at night. And you can also pick up the kids variant that comes with extra security features to keep your kids safe and under control.

Echo Auto (2nd Gen)
Best for your car

Don’t leave home without Alexa

$35 $55 Save $20

Most cars today arrive with great tools and features that make them better and smarter than before, but it’s always great to have Alexa ride with you so you can keep tabs on everything.
 

Pros
  • Smaller than its predecessor
  • Easy to install
  • Alexa on the go
Cons
  • Doesn't offer new features
  • Uses your phone's data plan
$35 at Amazon

The Echo Auto may not seem like the best option for those with newer cars since these vehicles usually arrive with tech like Android Auto and CarPlay that give you access to voice commands and touch-sensitive screens to interact with apps. However, there are plenty of vehicles that don’t include any of those features and might not even have Bluetooth support, which is exactly why Amazon Echo Auto is a great option. It immediately makes your car smarter by adding Alexa support and some convenient voice commands to your ride.

The new Echo Auto is not a smart speaker; it just comes with a microphone that connects to your car. Still, it will make your ride smarter. You can tell Alexa to play your favorite music, keep you updated with the latest news and sports scores, and even control your devices at home, meaning you can easily turn on or off your lights while on the road. It's quite convenient if you’re used to using Alexa as your main digital assistant.

You can easily set it up via Bluetooth connection or with the auxiliary cable that comes with your purchase. This will also allow you to connect Alexa with Android Auto or Apple’s CarPlay. Then it will ask you to configure different functions like calls, texts, directions, and media, which is great if you’re subscribed to Amazon’s Prime services, Spotify, and more.

I use mine to ask Alexa to play my favorite tunes, read my notifications, and add stuff to my shopping list. Still, there are many other ways to take advantage of Alexa in your car, as it continues to be one of the best and most efficient digital assistants you can use. Just don’t get too upset if your device loses connectivity while hitting the road, as data will be affected according to your company’s coverage.

Source: Amazon
Amazon Echo Pop
Best for small spaces

It will fit anywhere you want

$18 $40 Save $22

Amazon's smallest smart speaker will be perfect for small rooms and tight spaces as it won't take up too much space and will still deliver nice audio.

Pros
  • Small and compact footprint
  • Several color options
Cons
  • Not the best audio quality
  • Would've loved color-matching power cable
$18 at Amazon

Amazon’s most affordable smart speaker comes with a highly compact design that makes it perfect for any tight spot. It comes in four different color variants, and its $40 price tag makes it also the company’s most affordable option for those interested in having a first experience with Alexa’s ecosystem. You will also receive excellent audio that will be great for bedrooms and small spaces, and Alexa support will also let you control your favorite tunes from your preferred music streaming services. You can also connect to this speaker via Bluetooth in case you want to stream directly to your speaker.

Amazon Echo Pop will also let you control your smart home devices, so you can ask Alexa to clean your room if you have an Alexa-compatible robot vacuum or tell her to turn off the lights before you go to bed. And since we’re talking about lights, you will also be pleased to know that this smart speaker will only allow Alexa to listen to your queries and commands until it hears you say “Alexa” and the light turns blue. You can also turn off the mic on your smart speaker with a physical off button for added privacy.

Echo Buds (2nd Gen)
Best headphones

Affordable audio you can take anywhere you go

$65 $120 Save $55

Amazon's Echo Buds with active noise canceling are an excellent option for anyone who wants great audio and long-lasting battery life on a budget.

  • Great audio and noise cancelling
  • Decent battery life
Cons

Echo Glow

$17 $30 Save $13

All these drivers are powered by a 330W amp with a 24-bit DAC.

One of the best things about the Echo Studio is that it's a standalone speaker that works independently without relying on other Echo speakers, unlike the Echo Sub. It comes with full support for Alexa, meaning you can control your smart home devices or play music and get other information with just your voice. The Echo Studio even has the Zigbee hub built in that lets you directly connect smart home devices.

Amazon Echo Show 8 (3rd Gen, 2023)
Best smart display

Perfect size, solid features

$105 $150 Save $45

Amazon's third-generation Echo Show 8 is a new and powerful smart display that features a better display, improved audio, and all the features you get with the Alexa digital assistant that will let you control your smart home via easy voice commands.

Pros
  • Improved audio with Spatial Audio
  • Faster processor
Cons
  • A bit expensive for most
$105 at Amazon

The Echo Show 8 is one of my favorite Echo products, and it was one of the ones that received a very important upgrade, as this new smart display now includes excellent features that set it apart from other smart displays on the market. The third-generation Echo Show 8 now arrives with improved audio performance thanks to spatial audio, room acoustic sensing, and noise reduction technology that will make it sound better than ever before.

The Echo Show 8 also comes with an 8-inch HD touchscreen and a 13MP camera that will make your video calls more enjoyable. Also, you get a built-in smart home hub for seamless connectivity with Alexa-compatible devices and products, meaning that you can easily connect and control your lights, alarms, cameras, smart TVs, and more. It also features an improved processor with faster responses and an updated design with edge-to-edge glass and a centered camera. It also comes with a new $150 price tag, but it’s worth every penny.

Remember that the Echo Show 8 will also connect directly to other Echo devices to deliver a better media experience, with room-filling sound and two different color options to choose from. And it comes with multiple layers of privacy controls, including a mic/camera off button and a built-in camera shutter for ease of mind.

Source: Amazon
Echo Show 5 (3rd Gen)
Best for your nightstand

Bedside table smart display with Alexa

$40 $90 Save $50

The third-generation Amazon Echo Show 5 is one of Amazon's latest devices, which comes with several upgrades that make it the perfect companion for your bedside table.

Pros
  • Compact design
  • Improved audio and display
  • An affordable smart display
Cons
  • Not the best for video calls
$40 at Amazon

The Echo Show 5 is another device that received a facelift and some other improvements. This third-generation smart display is perfect for your nightstand. It comes with a more budget-friendly price tag, and a smaller 5.5-inch display that will let you check your notifications, and any other info that’s relevant for you. And don’t be fooled by its size, as it is a small but mighty smart display that will let you do everything you can do with its larger variant thanks to Alexa.

Amazon’s new Echo Show 5 comes in three different color variations, and you can even get yours with an adjustable stand if you’re willing to pay a bit more. This smart display comes with improved audio and better voice clarity, deeper bass, additional microphone for improved Alexa responsiveness, a new and faster processor with the company’s Az22 Neural Edge, an improved screen for a better viewing experience at night. And you can also pick up the kids variant that comes with extra security features to keep your kids safe and under control.

Echo Auto (2nd Gen)
Best for your car

Don’t leave home without Alexa

$35 $55 Save $20

Most cars today arrive with great tools and features that make them better and smarter than before, but it’s always great to have Alexa ride with you so you can keep tabs on everything.
 

Pros
  • Smaller than its predecessor
  • Easy to install
  • Alexa on the go
Cons
  • Doesn't offer new features
  • Uses your phone's data plan
$35 at Amazon

The Echo Auto may not seem like the best option for those with newer cars since these vehicles usually arrive with tech like Android Auto and CarPlay that give you access to voice commands and touch-sensitive screens to interact with apps. However, there are plenty of vehicles that don’t include any of those features and might not even have Bluetooth support, which is exactly why Amazon Echo Auto is a great option. It immediately makes your car smarter by adding Alexa support and some convenient voice commands to your ride.

The new Echo Auto is not a smart speaker; it just comes with a microphone that connects to your car. Still, it will make your ride smarter. You can tell Alexa to play your favorite music, keep you updated with the latest news and sports scores, and even control your devices at home, meaning you can easily turn on or off your lights while on the road. It's quite convenient if you’re used to using Alexa as your main digital assistant.

You can easily set it up via Bluetooth connection or with the auxiliary cable that comes with your purchase. This will also allow you to connect Alexa with Android Auto or Apple’s CarPlay. Then it will ask you to configure different functions like calls, texts, directions, and media, which is great if you’re subscribed to Amazon’s Prime services, Spotify, and more.

I use mine to ask Alexa to play my favorite tunes, read my notifications, and add stuff to my shopping list. Still, there are many other ways to take advantage of Alexa in your car, as it continues to be one of the best and most efficient digital assistants you can use. Just don’t get too upset if your device loses connectivity while hitting the road, as data will be affected according to your company’s coverage.

Source: Amazon
Amazon Echo Pop
Best for small spaces

It will fit anywhere you want

$18 $40 Save $22

Amazon's smallest smart speaker will be perfect for small rooms and tight spaces as it won't take up too much space and will still deliver nice audio.

Pros
  • Small and compact footprint
  • Several color options
Cons
  • Not the best audio quality
  • Would've loved color-matching power cable
$18 at Amazon

Amazon’s most affordable smart speaker comes with a highly compact design that makes it perfect for any tight spot. It comes in four different color variants, and its $40 price tag makes it also the company’s most affordable option for those interested in having a first experience with Alexa’s ecosystem. You will also receive excellent audio that will be great for bedrooms and small spaces, and Alexa support will also let you control your favorite tunes from your preferred music streaming services. You can also connect to this speaker via Bluetooth in case you want to stream directly to your speaker.

Amazon Echo Pop will also let you control your smart home devices, so you can ask Alexa to clean your room if you have an Alexa-compatible robot vacuum or tell her to turn off the lights before you go to bed. And since we’re talking about lights, you will also be pleased to know that this smart speaker will only allow Alexa to listen to your queries and commands until it hears you say “Alexa” and the light turns blue. You can also turn off the mic on your smart speaker with a physical off button for added privacy.

Echo Buds (2nd Gen)
Best headphones

Affordable audio you can take anywhere you go

$65 $120 Save $55

Amazon's Echo Buds with active noise canceling are an excellent option for anyone who wants great audio and long-lasting battery life on a budget. 

Pros
  • Great audio and noise cancelling
  • Decent battery life
Cons
  • Registration with Alexa app could be optional
$65 at Amazon

There are tons of amazing wireless earbuds on the market, but Amazon has a great pair as well, as the company’s Echo Buds with active noise canceling will deliver excellent audio quality without breaking the bank. These wireless earbuds were launched back in 2021, which also means that they’ve become more affordable with time, making them more compelling for those interested in picking up a pair of noise-canceling earbuds with Alexa support.

The Echo Buds with Active Noise Cancellation launched with a $120 price tag, but you can now easily pick up a pair for less than $70 with Amazon’s constant offers. Either way, you would be taking an excellent product with premium speakers to deliver crisp, balanced sound, and you can also personalize your audio for a unique hearing experience. They’re also quite compact and easy to wear, so you won’t have issues using them for extended periods of time, which will be limited to five hours of continuous music playback. You can also quickly charge them to get up to two hours of playback after a short 15-minute charge.

The best part about these wireless earbuds is that they are compatible with iOS and Android devices. You will only need to have a device with Android 8 or iOS 14 or later and activate them in the Alexa app, where you will also be able to mute the mics to keep your earbuds from listening to anything you have to say.

Echo Glow
Best Night light
$17 $30 Save $13

Amazon's Echo Glow will require a compatible Echo smart speaker to shine. Control your brightness, colors, and more via voice control to give your kids one of the best night lamps on the market.

Pros
  • Compact design with bright lights
  • Your kids will love it
Cons
  • Needs a compatible Echo smart speaker to work
  • No music sync
$17 at Amazon

The Echo Glow is another excellent product from Amazon, but it’s one of the few devices that needs another Echo device to work as intended. The Echo Glow is a multicolor bright lamp that requires a compatible Alexa device to operate. It is compatible with basically everything that came after the third-generation Echo Dot smart speaker. You will use your smart speaker to turn the Echo Glow on or off, control the color and brightness of your lamp with voice commands, and make it the perfect companion for your kids.

You can also create helpful routines reminding you to go to bed, take your pills, or start cooking when colors change. Setting a rainbow timer is also a nice option to give your kids time to get ready for school or to get them to do anything else. It comes with a compact footprint that will make it easier for you to set it up anywhere you want, and it also features an action button on the back that will let you interact with this fun little night light. I would only recommend you wait for Black Friday savings or Prime Day deals to pick one up, as paying $30 for a night light might not be for everyone.

Best Amazon Echo devices in 2023: Final thoughts

Amazon, as we mentioned earlier, has a lot of Echo devices in its lineup, but we've managed to highlight the best ones you can consider right now. Out of all the options mentioned in this collection, we'd say the Amazon Echo (4th Gen) is the best for most users. It's a great-sounding speaker that can fill your room with good music, and it also supports Alexa to control your smart home devices. Amazon has yet to release an updated fifth-generation model of the regular Echo speaker, so this one remains our best pick for now.

Amazon Echo (4th Gen)
Best overall
$55 $100 Save $45

Amazon's 4th Echo speaker remains our pick for the best overall Echo device you can buy right now. It's due for an upgrade this year, but you can't go wrong with this model too.

$55 at Amazon

Those who are relatively new to the world of Amazon's Echo speakers should consider buying the Echo Dot (5th Gen). It's a solid speaker to kick things off, and it's particularly good when you pair it with a couple of smart home products like smart bulbs and start playing with the idea of controlling your devices with an Echo before upgrading to a bigger, more expensive speaker.

item.title: Get the new Fitbit Charge 6 and more at their lowest prices ever for early Black Friday
item.itunes:title: Get the new Fitbit Charge 6 and more at their lowest prices ever for early Black Friday
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Black Friday is just around the corner, but we've already been seeing major price drops from Amazon, Best Buy, and many other retailers. Since fitness tracker accessories can be pricey during the year, now is a really golden opportunity to buy one, and Fitbit has brought massive discounts on its trackers, including the brand new Fitbit Charge 6.

Fitbit Charge 6

Source: Fitbit 
Fitbit Charge 6
$100 $160 Save $60

It might've just come out but you can already find the excellent Fitbit Charge 6 at its lowest price ever. There are three amazing colors to choose from, and access to all the health features you could want.

$100 at Silver case w/ White band $100 at Black Case w/ Black Band $100 at Gold Case w/ Red Band

The Fitbit Charge 6 is an all-inclusive fitness tracker coming with more than 40 exercise modes, built-in GPS, automatic exercise tracking, a seven-day battery life, and much more. With ECG and oxygen saturation monitoring, it’s one of the best fitness trackers on the market. What's more, you even get access to smartwatch features like NFC payments.

Fitbit Inspire 3

Fitbit Inspire 3

This Black Friday, get the Fitbit Inspire 3 for just $70, which is normally priced at $100. You can choose from two colors: Lilac Bliss and Morning Glow.

$70 at Amazon

The Fitbit Inspire 3 is a more basic fitness tracker, but it still has over 20 exercise modes, menstrual health tracking, and all-day activity and heart-rate tracking. There’s also a smart sleep management system to help you sleep better. With a massive 10-day battery life, 50-meter water resistance, and a low weight, it can be an ideal partner for your fitness journey.

Now is your chance to get your hands on one of the best fitness trackers out there! And that, too, for less than $100. But make sure you get it fast, as most Black Friday deals don't last for long.

From some of the best kids smartwatches to the best smartwatches in general, Fitbit is one of the top companies when it comes to making health and wellness-tracking wearables. The Fitbit Charge 5 is one of the best fitness trackers and continues to be a dependable device even today. If you already own a Charge 5 and are now starting to get a little bored with it, it’s time to try some new bands to make it exciting again. The Charge 5 has been officially discontinued as it was replaced by the Charge 6 this year, but thankfully, the bands are interchangeable across both devices. If you’ve not bothered getting a new band for your Charge 5 yet, here’s a list of some of the top bands that are worth checking out.

item.title: Las Vegas F1 Grand Prix livestream: How to watch from anywhere
item.itunes:title: Las Vegas F1 Grand Prix livestream: How to watch from anywhere
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
This weekend, the F1 Grand Prix races to a new stop on the circuit, Las Vegas. It's the first time the motorsport has shredded rubber on the City of Sin's tarmac since 1982, and it promises to be a fantastic race.

You can't go to Vegas without spending some time on the Strip, and even F1 drivers aren't immune to the draw of the neon lights. The newly constructed circuit zooms down some city streets towards the new Sphere arena, then transitions to tearing down the Strip near many of the big casinos. Bright lights, big attractions and big speeds, what a weekend.

When and where?

The Las Vegas F1 Grand Prix is set to race off from the starting line on Saturday, November 18. Coverage begins at 9:55 p.m. PT, 12:55 a.m. ET (Sunday), and 5:55 a.m. GMT (Sunday) for those looking to tune in from around the world.

How to watch the F1 Grand Prix Las Vegas circuit from anywhere

Whether you're a big sports fan or just have weekly shows that you don't want to miss, traveling can throw off your routine big time. Not all channels are available everywhere, and local programming will vary based on where you are. Don't worry, though, a VPN can help get easy access to all your favorite shows in just minutes. Using one of the best VPNs is an easy way to not only change your location virtually, but also to add an extra layer of security to your internet connection, which is especially useful when traveling.

Using a VPN service, like our favorite service ExpressVPN, makes it simple to change your location in just a few clicks. Once your locations are properly set, you'll be able to access all the programming that you're looking for, whether it's a new show or sporting event. Right now, you can get 3 free months of ExpressVPN when you sign up for the service's annual subscription, bringing the total for 15 months down to $100. You also get a 30-day money-back guarantee, so if something doesn't go according to plan you'll still be covered. Between the 24/7 support that's offered, great range of server locations and the consistency in speed and performance, ExpressVPN is easy to recommend. Many VPN services seem similar on the surface, but few, if any, offer the total package of ExpressVPN.

If you're looking for other options, be sure to check out all the best VPN deals that are available right now.

Source: ExpressVPN
ExpressVPN

ExpressVPN is one of our favorite VPN services that's available right now. Not only is it easy to use, but the company provides great customer support, and backs it all with a 30-day money-back guarantee.

See at ExpressVPN

How to stream the Las Vegas Grand Prix in the US

If you're in the US and want to tune in and watch the F1 Grand Prix from Las Vegas live, Sling TV is your best bet. The event is streaming exclusively on ESPN+ in the US, and that means Sling TV's Orange package is the most affordable place to get it. You can sign up for just a month of access, which will be billed at $20 instead of the usual $40, but you'll want to remember to cancel it before the renewal date, or else you'll be billed at the normal rate.

ESPN+ is the home of F1 action in the US, which means you can also watch on many other streaming services. Hulu with Live TV carries ESPN+, and you'll also get Disney+ (with ads) so you can catch up on Loki and the rest of the MCU between races. And YouTube TV also has ESPN+, if you've already subscribed to that because you wanted NFL Sunday Ticket.

  • Source: Sling TV
    Sling TV

    For only $20 a month for the first three months, Sling TV's Orange package is a great deal for F1 fans, and many other sporting events.

    See at Sling TV
  • Source: Hulu
    Hulu

    Hulu with Live TV also carries ESPN+ (with ads) so you can catch up on TV, Disney+ shows, and of course, all the blisteringly fast F1 Grand Prix action.

    See at Hulu
  • Source: Alphabet
    YouTube TV

    YouTube TV also carries ESPN+, so every minute of every F1 race will be shown as part of your subscription. This is the only place to get NFL Sunday Ticket too, for all the gridiron action after the excitement of the F1 has subsided.

    See at Google

How to watch the F1 Grand Prix in the UK

In the UK, your best bet is to try and catch the F1 from Las Vegas live by tuning in and watching it on Sky Sports F1. That's the traditional place to catch any racing in the UK, whether that's on two, four, or no wheels at all. Or, you can subscribe to a Now Sports Pass, which gives you the full Sky Sports experience for £35 a month or £12 a day. The only thing here is that you'll be limited to 1080p content, as you need the cable package for 4K coverage.

Source: Sky
Sky Sports

Sky Sports is your best bet for the F1, and any other type of motorsport in the UK. Remember you'll need a Sky Box and Sky subscription first, or you can sign up for Now Sports for the day of the race for £12.

See at Sky Sports

Sky Sports

Sky Sports is your best bet for the F1, and any other type of motorsport in the UK. Remember you'll need a Sky Box and Sky subscription first, or you can sign up for Now Sports for the day of the race for £12.

See at Sky Sports
item.content:encoded:
This weekend, the F1 Grand Prix races to a new stop on the circuit, Las Vegas. It's the first time the motorsport has shredded rubber on the City of Sin's tarmac since 1982, and it promises to be a fantastic race.

You can't go to Vegas without spending some time on the Strip, and even F1 drivers aren't immune to the draw of the neon lights. The newly constructed circuit zooms down some city streets towards the new Sphere arena, then transitions to tearing down the Strip near many of the big casinos. Bright lights, big attractions and big speeds, what a weekend.

When and where?

The Las Vegas F1 Grand Prix is set to race off from the starting line on Saturday, November 18. Coverage begins at 9:55 p.m. PT, 12:55 a.m. ET (Sunday), and 5:55 a.m. GMT (Sunday) for those looking to tune in from around the world.

How to watch the F1 Grand Prix Las Vegas circuit from anywhere

Whether you're a big sports fan or just have weekly shows that you don't want to miss, traveling can throw off your routine big time. Not all channels are available everywhere, and local programming will vary based on where you are. Don't worry, though, a VPN can help get easy access to all your favorite shows in just minutes. Using one of the best VPNs is an easy way to not only change your location virtually, but also to add an extra layer of security to your internet connection, which is especially useful when traveling.

Using a VPN service, like our favorite service ExpressVPN, makes it simple to change your location in just a few clicks. Once your locations are properly set, you'll be able to access all the programming that you're looking for, whether it's a new show or sporting event. Right now, you can get 3 free months of ExpressVPN when you sign up for the service's annual subscription, bringing the total for 15 months down to $100. You also get a 30-day money-back guarantee, so if something doesn't go according to plan you'll still be covered. Between the 24/7 support that's offered, great range of server locations and the consistency in speed and performance, ExpressVPN is easy to recommend. Many VPN services seem similar on the surface, but few, if any, offer the total package of ExpressVPN.

If you're looking for other options, be sure to check out all the best VPN deals that are available right now.

Source: ExpressVPN
ExpressVPN

ExpressVPN is one of our favorite VPN services that's available right now. Not only is it easy to use, but the company provides great customer support, and backs it all with a 30-day money-back guarantee.

See at ExpressVPN

How to stream the Las Vegas Grand Prix in the US

If you're in the US and want to tune in and watch the F1 Grand Prix from Las Vegas live, Sling TV is your best bet. The event is streaming exclusively on ESPN+ in the US, and that means Sling TV's Orange package is the most affordable place to get it. You can sign up for just a month of access, which will be billed at $20 instead of the usual $40, but you'll want to remember to cancel it before the renewal date, or else you'll be billed at the normal rate.

ESPN+ is the home of F1 action in the US, which means you can also watch on many other streaming services. Hulu with Live TV carries ESPN+, and you'll also get Disney+ (with ads) so you can catch up on Loki and the rest of the MCU between races. And YouTube TV also has ESPN+, if you've already subscribed to that because you wanted NFL Sunday Ticket.

  • Source: Sling TV
    Sling TV

    For only $20 a month for the first three months, Sling TV's Orange package is a great deal for F1 fans, and many other sporting events.

    See at Sling TV
  • Source: Hulu
    Hulu

    Hulu with Live TV also carries ESPN+ (with ads) so you can catch up on TV, Disney+ shows, and of course, all the blisteringly fast F1 Grand Prix action.

    See at Hulu
  • Source: Alphabet
    YouTube TV

    YouTube TV also carries ESPN+, so every minute of every F1 race will be shown as part of your subscription. This is the only place to get NFL Sunday Ticket too, for all the gridiron action after the excitement of the F1 has subsided.

    See at Google

How to watch the F1 Grand Prix in the UK

In the UK, your best bet is to try and catch the F1 from Las Vegas live by tuning in and watching it on Sky Sports F1. That's the traditional place to catch any racing in the UK, whether that's on two, four, or no wheels at all. Or, you can subscribe to a Now Sports Pass, which gives you the full Sky Sports experience for £35 a month or £12 a day. The only thing here is that you'll be limited to 1080p content, as you need the cable package for 4K coverage.

Source: Sky
Sky Sports

Sky Sports is your best bet for the F1, and any other type of motorsport in the UK. Remember you'll need a Sky Box and Sky subscription first, or you can sign up for Now Sports for the day of the race for £12.

See at Sky Sports

item.title: Best Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5 cases in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5 cases in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 5 is officially one of the best phones released this year. Samsung played it safe this year, with minor adjustments to the overall design, while fixing one of the main complaints about foldable phones. That’s the crease, which is drastically reduced as the new teardrop-shaped hinge lets the screen bend over a larger radius. The design also means the two halves are parallel when closed, reducing the thickness when in your pocket. You’ll still want a case to protect your $1,800 investment, and we’ve rounded up the best on the market to choose from.
